title: My Steak Is Too Juicy / My Lobster Is Too Buttery
about: My Steak Is Too Juicy and My Lobster Is Too Buttery are catchphrases used online, often together, to mockingly suggest that someone is ungrateful and complaining about having too much of a good thing, essentially claiming that their argument is invalid and the thing they're complaining about should actually be enjoyed. The slang expressions gained prominence throughout 2025 as a reaction on sites like TikTok, X / Twitter and Reddit. The phrase seemingly originates from an iFunny comment reading, "Oh no, my lobster is too buttery and my steak is too juicy!" posted in response to a text-post reading, "Everyone wants a nerd girl till she comes out in her fantastic 4 jammies and yells it's slobbering time pre-blowjob."
origin:
On March 2nd, 2025, iFunny[1] user Andr3wTheGr8 posted a screenshot of a comment with 2,200 likes left on an unknown iFunny user's post. The post by the unknown user is a text graphic reading, "Everyone wants a nerd girl till she comes out in her fantastic 4 jammies and yells it's slobbering time pre-blowjob."

The comment, left by user Sagia, reads, "'Oh no, my lobster is too buttery and my steak is too juicy!'" suggesting that the described scenario of a nerd girl yelling "it's slobbering time" is not a problem, as portrayed, but something to celebrate or be grateful for. It is unclear which iFunny user posted the text-graphic or how much interaction it or the comment by Sagia received.
